title: dryopteris tetragona var. guadalupensis
intro: Dryopteris tetragona var. guadalupensis, also known as Guadalupe shield fern, is a variety of the Dryopteris tetragona fern species native to the mountainous regions of Mexico and the southwestern United States. It is a medicinal plant used for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and skin irritations. The plant's medicinal properties can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, which have been shown to have a range of health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Dryopteris tetragona var. guadalupensis can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its extracts are also used in herbal supplements to harness its medicinal properties.
